title: T-076 — cross-org grant rejection (assertActorBelongsToOrg) + integration test (FT-016)
prompt:
# T-076 — cross-org grant rejection (assertActorBelongsToOrg) + integration test (FT-016)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.x
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-016** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P1** (S effort)

## Goal
Add an explicit cross-org guard to grantRole and prove rejection with an integration test.

## Implementation contract
1. New helper `assertActorBelongsToOrg(actorUserId, orgId)` queries user_org_memberships and throws if no row.
2. grantRole calls the assertion before issuing any GRANT.
3. Integration test seeds two orgs, one actor in org A, attempts grant in org B; asserts rejection.

## Files (scope)
- packages/rbac/src/assert-actor-belongs-to-org.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/src/grant.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/cross-org-grant.integration.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given actor in org A and target user in org B, when grantRole(actor, target, role, orgB) runs, then it throws 'cross_org_grant_denied' and no audit_events row is created.
2. Given actor in org A and target user in org A, when grantRole runs, then the role is granted and an audit_events row is created.
3. Given the actor user does not exist, when grantRole runs, then it throws with 'actor_unknown'.

## Test strategy
- RED: write cross-org-grant.integration.test.ts seeding two orgs + actor and asserting rejection before implementation.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac test cross-org-grant`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not log the actor's full email in the rejection error (PII).
- Do not skip the actor-unknown branch.
- Do not use raw new pg.Pool.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Add assertActorBelongsToOrg(actorUserId, orgId) guard to grantRole and prove with an integration test that cross-org grants are rejected.
pipeline_inputs.details: grantRole currently trusts the caller-provided orgId. An actor in org A could theoretically grant a role in org B if the caller chain bypasses RLS. Add an explicit assertion that the actor belongs to the target org.
out_of_scope: Do not refactor grantRole to use the app-pool here (FT-017).

Given actor in org A and target user in org A, when grantRole runs, then the role is granted and an audit_events row is created.
Given the actor user does not exist, when grantRole runs, then it throws with 'actor_unknown'.
